threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Night Tube' like it’s some trendy new concept instead of the chaotic, sleep-deprived reality it is.
Heard a guy on the 2 bus chatting about how he saw a fox stealing chips from a kebab shop. Just another night in Brixton, I guess.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard a toddler argue with their parent in Turkish about which döner shop was better—classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bar that serves Schnitzel with vegan alternatives, blending tradition and modern tastes in a way that feels uniquely Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to pack an umbrella for the fog that rolls in every evening, but leaves it at home until then.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a musician playing a haunting tune on the 31. Every rider just paused, lost in that moment—guess we all needed a break from the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's squares are just for pictures, but locals actually use them to unwind after work. It's a different vibe come sunset.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still look for a metro map on the wall instead of pulling it up on their phone.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you order a bagel and ask for it toasted. The locals just look at you like you've sprouted wings.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a kebab as we rolled past the Windmill. Only in Brixton, right? They've got a way of turning late night drama into a snack stop.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a group of kids playing soccer in the street while a kebab shop's smoke wafted overhead—it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab stand with a rotating art exhibit above it. Who knew döner could be so cultured?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their patience at the BART delays—newbies just complain while we’re already scrolling through NextBus for alternatives.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught that bus this morning packed with folks, even had a guy playing the accordion. Only in SF can rush hour feel like a street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is always buzzing with parties, but locals know it’s the quiet corners and the scent of fresh paella on Sunday that make it special.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their bewilderment at the local shop owners insisting on greeting them with a hearty 'Hola!' before asking for their order.
